domain: Pakistani Saraiki dohra two-line parallel couplet (Sufi tradition; semantically redundant couplets share an anchor word via parallel grammatical structure)
specific_mechanism: Two parallel lines share grammatical scaffold; second-line content is decodable by anchoring to a shared key word from the first line. Stacked dohras provide N-way redundant attention to the same anchor.
llm_application: DOHRA-DECODE: N-branch parallel decoder where each branch is conditioned on the same shared anchor token; branches generate semantically-redundant chunks in parallel; majority-vote or anchor-grounded reconciliation at the output. Different from PASTA-style semantic-independence parallelism: branches are deliberately REDUNDANT around the anchor, not independent.
novelty_claim: PASTA decodes semantically INDEPENDENT chunks in parallel. Speculative decoding uses a draft model to predict ahead. DOHRA-DECODE is structurally different: N parallel branches each conditioned on a shared anchor token to produce redundant chunks for majority-vote consistency. Closest is anchor-token decoding (ICLR 2025) which manipulates the anchor for hallucination control, not N-branch parallel voting.
